description.content:
Returns whether a user is watching a piece of content. Choose the user by
doing one of the following:

- Specify a user via a query parameter: Use the `accountId` to identify the user.
- Do not specify a user: The currently logged-in user will be used.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
'Confluence Administrator' global permission if specifying a user, otherwise
permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

variable.description:
(Required) The ID of the content to be queried for whether the specified user is
watching it.

response.name: Returned if the requested watch status is returned.

variable.description:
(Required) The ID of the content to be queried for whether the specified user is
watching it.

response.name:
Returned if;

- The calling user does not have permission to view the
content.
- A user is specified via a query parameter and the calling user is
not a Confluence administrator.
- No content exists for the specified `contentId`.

variable.description:
(Required) The ID of the content to be queried for whether the specified user is
watching it.

response.name: Returned if no `contentId` is specified.

variable.description:
(Required) The ID of the content to be queried for whether the specified user is
watching it.

description.content:
Adds a user as a watcher to a piece of content. Choose the user by doing
one of the following:

- Specify a user via a query parameter: Use the `accountId` to identify the user.
- Do not specify a user: The currently logged-in user will be used.

Note, you must add the `X-Atlassian-Token: no-check` header when making a
request, as this operation has XSRF protection.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
'Confluence Administrator' global permission if specifying a user, otherwise
permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the content to add the watcher to.

response.name:
Returned if the watcher was successfully created. No response body is
returned.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the content to add the watcher to.

response.name:
Returned if;

- The `X-Atlassian-Token: no-check` header is not specified.
- The calling user does not have permission to view the
content.
- A user is specified via a query parameter and the calling user is
not a Confluence administrator.
- No content exists for the specified `contentId`.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the content to add the watcher to.

response.name: Returned if no `contentId` is specified.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the content to add the watcher to.

description.content:
Returns the properties for a user as list of property keys. For more information
about user properties, see [Confluence entity properties](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/confluence-entity-properties/).
`Note`, these properties stored against a user are on a Confluence site level and not space/content level.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description: The starting index of the returned properties.

The maximum number of properties to return per page.
Note, this may be restricted by fixed system limits.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

response.name: Returned if the requested properties are returned.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

response.name: Returned if request has malformed syntax or userId is not a valid user id.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

response.name: Returned if the authentication credentials are incorrect or missing from the request.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

response.name: Returned if the user does not have the correct permissions.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

response.name: Returned if there is no user with the given user ID.

variable.description: (Required) The account ID of the user to be queried for its properties.

description.content:
Returns the property corresponding to `key` for a user. For more information
about user properties, see [Confluence entity properties](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/confluence-entity-properties/).
`Note`, these properties stored against a user are on a Confluence site level and not space/content level.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

(Required) The key of the user property.

description.content:
Updates a property for the given user. Note, you cannot update the key of a user property, only the value.
For more information about user properties, see
[Confluence entity properties](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/confluence-entity-properties/).
`Note`, these properties stored against a user are on a Confluence site level and not space/content level.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

variable.description:
(Required) The account ID of the user. The accountId uniquely identifies the user across all Atlassian
products. For example, 384093:32b4d9w0-f6a5-3535-11a3-9c8c88d10192
(Required) The key of the user property.

response.name: Returned if the user property is updated.

item.name: Atlassian Create User Property by Key

request.name: Atlassian Create User Property by Key

description.content:
Creates a property for a user. For more information about user properties, see [Confluence entity properties]
(https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/confluence-entity-properties/).
`Note`, these properties stored against a user are on a Confluence site level and not space/content level.

`Note:` the number of properties which could be created per app in a tenant for each user might be
restricted by fixed system limits.
**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

description.content:
Returns information about how anonymous users are represented, like the
profile picture and display name.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description:
A multi-value parameter indicating which properties of the user to
expand.

  - `operations` returns the operations that the user is allowed to do.

response.name: Returned if the anonymous user representation is returned.

Returned if the calling user does not have permission to use
Confluence.

description.content:
Returns the currently logged-in user. This includes information about
the user, like the display name, userKey, account ID, profile picture,
and more.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description:
A multi-value parameter indicating which properties of the user to
expand.

  - `operations` returns the operations that the user is allowed to do.
  - `personalSpace` returns the user's personal space, if it exists.
  - `isExternalCollaborator` returns whether the user is an external collaborator user.

response.name: Returned if the current user is returned.

Returned if the calling user does not have permission to use
Confluence.

item.name: Atlassian Get Group Memberships for User

request.name: Atlassian Get Group Memberships for User

description.content:
Returns the groups that a user is a member of.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description:
(Required) The account ID of the user. The accountId uniquely identifies the user across all Atlassian products.
For example, `384093:32b4d9w0-f6a5-3535-11a3-9c8c88d10192`.
The starting index of the returned groups.

The maximum number of groups to return per page.
Note, this may be restricted by fixed system limits.

response.name: Returned if the requested groups are returned.

Returned if the calling user does not have permission to use
Confluence.

item.name: Atlassian Get Multiple Users Using Ids

request.name: Atlassian Get Multiple Users Using Ids

description.content:
Returns user details for the ids provided in request.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description: (Required) A list of accountId's of users to be returned.

A multi-value parameter indicating which properties of the user to
expand.

  - `operations` returns the operations that the user is allowed to do.
  - `personalSpace` returns the user's personal space, if it exists.
  - `isExternalCollaborator` returns whether the user is an external collaborator user.

The maximum number of results returned.
Currently API returns 200 results max.
If more that 200 ids are passed first 200 will be returned.

response.name: Returned if, the list of users is returned.

Returned if the calling user does not have permission to use
Confluence.

description.content:
Returns a user's email address. This API is only available to apps approved by
Atlassian, according to these [guidelines](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/guidelines-for-requesting-access-to-email-address/27603).

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description:
(Required) The account ID of the user, which uniquely identifies the user across all Atlassian products.
For example, `384093:32b4d9w0-f6a5-3535-11a3-9c8c88d10192`. Required.

response.name: Returned if the requested user's email is returned.
Returned if the calling app is not approved to use this API.

Returned if the authentication credentials are incorrect or missing
from the request.
Returned if a user with the given accountId does not exist.
Indicates that the API is not currently enabled.

item.name: Atlassian Get User Email Addresses in Batch

request.name: Atlassian Get User Email Addresses in Batch

description.content:
Returns user email addresses for a set of accountIds. This API is only available to apps approved by
Atlassian, according to these [guidelines](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/guidelines-for-requesting-access-to-email-address/27603).

Any accounts which are not available will not be included in the result.

**[Permissions](https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/_AozKw) required**:
Permission to access the Confluence site ('Can use' global permission).

query.description: (Required) The account IDs of the users.

response.name: Returned if the request is successful.
Returned if the calling app is not approved to use this API.

Returned if the authentication credentials are incorrect or missing
from the request.
Indicates that the API is not currently enabled.

description.content: The Atlassian Confluence User API is a powerful tool that allows developers to interact with user data within the Confluence platform. With this API, developers can create, read, update, and delete user information, such as usernames, email addresses, and profile details. This API provides a seamless way to manage user accounts, permissions, and settings in Confluence, making it easier for administrators to control access and permissions for users.
